name: Scanpy (Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Analysis)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3-Clause); Fabian Theis / Helmholtz Munich

performance.notes: AnnData-backed; GPU via RAPIDS/cuML optional; 100K+ cell datasets common

agent_readiness.notes: Single-cell RNA-seq analysis standard for Python. import scanpy as sc. adata = sc.read_10x_mtx('filtered_matrix/'). sc.pp.filter_cells(adata, min_genes=200). sc.pp.filter_genes(adata, min_cells=3). sc.pp.calculate_qc_metrics(adata). sc.pp.normalize_total(adata, target_sum=1e4). sc.pp.log1p(adata). sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(adata, n_top_genes=2000). sc.pp.pca(adata). sc.pp.neighbors(adata). sc.tl.umap(adata). sc.tl.leiden(adata). sc.pl.umap(adata, color='leiden'). sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(adata, 'leiden'). AnnData format.

gotchas: Results are not reproducible by default — set random seeds explicitly
Many operations modify AnnData in place — be careful with copies
UMAP and clustering are stochastic — set random_state for reproducibility
Large datasets (>1M cells) require GPU or chunked processing
best_when: Single-cell RNA-seq preprocessing, clustering, and visualization
Leiden clustering and differential gene expression
10x Chromium / Seurat-compatible scRNA-seq pipelines in Python
avoid_when: Spatial transcriptomics beyond basic Scanpy (use Squidpy)
When Seurat (R) is the team standard
summary: Scanpy is the standard Python library for single-cell RNA-seq analysis. sc.read_10x_mtx(). filter_cells/genes. normalize_total. log1p. highly_variable_genes. pca. umap. leiden clustering. rank_genes_groups for marker genes. AnnData format. BSD-3 by Fabian Theis/Helmholtz. Standard Python scRNA-seq pipeline. In-place modifications — copy before processing. Set random_state for reproducibility.
